diive.core.dfun.frames¶

DATA FUNCTIONS: FRAMES¶

This module is part of the diive library: https://github.com/holukas/diive

Functions

add_continuous_record_number(df)

Add continuous record number as new column

aggregated_as_hires(aggregate_series, ...[, ...])

Calculate aggregated values and apply high-res timestamp

compare_len_header_vs_data(n_cols_data, ...)

Check whether there are more data columns than given in the header

convert_data_to_numeric(df)

Sanitize time series

convert_to_arrays(df, target_col[, ...])

Convert data from df to numpy arrays and prepare targets, features and their timestamp

detect_new_columns(df, other)

Detect columns in df that do not exist in other.

df_between_two_dates(df, start_date, end_date)

Get data for the time window, >= start date and <= end date

df_unique_values(df)

Return numpy array of unique values across all columns of a dataframe

flatten_multiindex_all_df_cols(df[, ...])

generate_flag_daynight(df[, flag_based_on, ...])

Add flag to indicate group membership: daytime or nighttime data

get_len_data(filepath[, skiprows, headerrows])

Check number of columns of the first data row after the header part

parse_header(filepath, varnames_row[, ...])

Read variable names and units from a csv file.

rename_cols(df, renaming_dict)

Rename columns in dataframe

rename_cols_to_multiindex(df, renaming_dict)

Rename columns (one-row header) in dataframe to multiindex (two-row header).

sort_multiindex_columns_names(df, priority_vars)

Sort column names, ascending, with priority vars at top

transform_yearmonth_matrix_to_longform(matrixdf)

Transform a year-month matrix into a long-form pandas Series with a datetime index.

trim_frame(df, var)

Trim start and end of dataframe, based on the first and last record of a specific variable.
diive.core.dfun.frames.compare_len_header_vs_data(n_cols_data, n_cols_header, varnames_list, varunits_list)[source]¶

Check whether there are more data columns than given in the header

If not checked, this would results in an error when reading the csv file with .read_csv, because the method expects an equal number of header and data columns. If this check is True, then the difference between the length of the first data row and the length of the header row(s) can be used to automatically generate names for the missing header columns.

Parameters:

  • n_cols_data (int)

  • n_cols_header (int)

  • varnames_list (list)

  • varunits_list (list)

diive.core.dfun.frames.trim_frame(df, var)[source]¶

Trim start and end of dataframe, based on the first and last record of a specific variable.

Only start and end of the dataframe are trimmed, other missing values of var are ignored.

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • var (str)

Example:

If df contains multiple variables with data between 05:00 and 06:00, but var is only available between 05:20 and 05:50, then df is trimmed: all data before 05:20 and after 05:50 are removed and the returned dataframe is thus shorter.

Args:

df: Dataframe that contains var. var: Name of the variable in df that is used to trim df.

Returns:

Trimmed dataframe.

diive.core.dfun.frames.detect_new_columns(df, other)[source]¶

Detect columns in df that do not exist in other.

Return type:

list

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • other (DataFrame)

diive.core.dfun.frames.aggregated_as_hires(aggregate_series, hires_timestamp, to_freq='D', to_agg='mean', interpolate_missing_vals=False, interpolation_lim=False)[source]¶

Calculate aggregated values and apply high-res timestamp

Example: half-hourly timestamp for daily maximum temperature

Return type:

Series

Parameters:

  • aggregate_series (Series)

  • to_freq (str)

  • to_agg (str)

  • interpolate_missing_vals (bool)

  • interpolation_lim (int)

diive.core.dfun.frames.rename_cols(df, renaming_dict)[source]¶

Rename columns in dataframe

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • renaming_dict (dict)

Args:

df: Data containing oldname column renaming_dict: Dictionary that contains old column names as its keys,

and new column names as values, e.g. {‘oldname’: ‘newname’, ‘oldname2’: ‘newname2’, …}.

Returns:

DataFrame

diive.core.dfun.frames.rename_cols_to_multiindex(df, renaming_dict)[source]¶

Rename columns (one-row header) in dataframe to multiindex (two-row header).

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • renaming_dict (dict)

Args:

df: Data containing oldname column renaming_dict: Dictionary that contains old column names as its keys,

and new column names with units as tuples:
e.g. {

‘oldname1’: (‘newname1’, ‘units1’), ‘oldname2’: (‘newname2’, ‘units2’), ‘oldname3’: (‘newname3’, ‘units3’), … }

Returns:

DataFrame

diive.core.dfun.frames.convert_data_to_numeric(df)[source]¶

Sanitize time series

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

df (DataFrame)

diive.core.dfun.frames.get_len_data(filepath, skiprows=None, headerrows=None)[source]¶

Check number of columns of the first data row after the header part

Parameters:

  • filepath (Path)

  • skiprows (list)

  • headerrows (list)

diive.core.dfun.frames.parse_header(filepath, varnames_row, varunits_row=None, skiprows=None, headerrows=None)[source]¶

Read variable names and units from a csv file.

Units are optional since they are not included in all data files.

Return type:

tuple[list, list, int]

Parameters:

  • filepath (Path)

  • varnames_row (int)

  • varunits_row (int)

  • skiprows (list)

  • headerrows (list)

Example:

In a file, if data start in row 7 and variable names are in row 2 with variable units in row 3 the settings are:

skiprows=[0, 1, 4, 5, 6] varnames_row=0 varnames_units=1

Args:

filepath: Path to the csv file. varnames_row: Row index of variable names after considering skiprows. varunits_row: Row index of variable units after considering skiprows. skiprows: Ignored rows at start of file.

For example: [0, 1, 2] would ignore the first 3 rows of the file.

headerrows: (deprecated)

Returns:

Lists of variable names and units, and integer of number of rows.

diive.core.dfun.frames.df_unique_values(df)[source]¶

Return numpy array of unique values across all columns of a dataframe

Parameters¶

df: pandas DataFrame

Returns¶

array

diive.core.dfun.frames.flatten_multiindex_all_df_cols(df, keep_first_row_only=False)[source]¶
Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • keep_first_row_only (bool)

diive.core.dfun.frames.df_between_two_dates(df, start_date, end_date, dropna_col=None, dropna=False)[source]¶

Get data for the time window, >= start date and <= end date

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • dropna_col (str)

  • dropna (bool)

Args:

df: start_date: end_date: dropna: Remove NaNs dropna_col: Column name in df that is checked for NaNs

Returns:

Data between start_date and end_date

diive.core.dfun.frames.sort_multiindex_columns_names(df, priority_vars)[source]¶

Sort column names, ascending, with priority vars at top

Files w/ many columns are otherwise hard to navigate.

This is trickier than anticipated (by me), b/c sorting by

data_df.sort_index(axis=1, inplace=True)

sorts the columns, but is case-sensitive, i.e. ‘Var2’ is placed before ‘var1’, yielding the order ‘Var2’, ‘var1’. However, we want it sorted like this: ‘var1’, ‘Var2’. Therefore, the columns are converted to a list, the list is then sorted ignoring case, and the sorted list is then used to define the column order in the df.

diive.core.dfun.frames.convert_to_arrays(df, target_col, complete_rows=True)[source]¶

Convert data from df to numpy arrays and prepare targets, features and their timestamp

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • target_col (str)

  • complete_rows (bool)

diive.core.dfun.frames.add_continuous_record_number(df)[source]¶

Add continuous record number as new column

Return type:

DataFrame

Parameters:

df (DataFrame)

diive.core.dfun.frames.generate_flag_daynight(df, flag_based_on='timestamp', ts_daytime_start_hour=7, ts_daytime_end_hour=19, col_thres_flagtrue='Larger Than Threshold', col_thres_flag_threshold=None)[source]¶

Add flag to indicate group membership: daytime or nighttime data

Parameters:

  • df (DataFrame)

  • flag_based_on (str)

  • ts_daytime_start_hour (int)

  • ts_daytime_end_hour (int)

  • col_thres_flagtrue (str)

  • col_thres_flag_threshold (float)

diive.core.dfun.frames.transform_yearmonth_matrix_to_longform(matrixdf, z_var_name='VALUE')[source]¶

Transform a year-month matrix into a long-form pandas Series with a datetime index.

Matrix Structure:

The input matrixdf must have:

  • Years as the index.

  • Months as the columns.

  • Numerical values representing the data.

Example:

MONTH 1 2 3 YEAR 1997 2.0 9.0 5.0 1998 10.0 1.0 19.0 1999 8.0 22.0 13.0

Transformation:

The function combines the year (from the index) and month (from the columns) to create a datetime index. The corresponding values from the matrix become the values of the resulting Series.

Example Output (with z_var_name as ‘VALUE’):

VALUE

TIMESTAMP 1997-01-01 2.0 1997-02-01 9.0 1997-03-01 5.0 …

Args:

matrixdf: pandas DataFrame with years as index and months as columns. z_var_name: (Optional) Name of the resulting pandas Series. Defaults to ‘VALUE’.

Returns:

pandas Series with a datetime index (‘YYYY-MM-01’ format, monthly start frequency ‘MS’) and values from the input matrix.

Return type:

Series

Parameters:

  • matrixdf (DataFrame)

  • z_var_name (str)
